calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Justin Shiau appeared via zoom for Karen Lee (petitioner).

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated December 7, 1981 is admitted to probate. Karen Park Lee is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $2,600,000.00 Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 18, 2027 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Frank Mahoney III is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Shiau for petitioner Karen Park Lee (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

The Court raised the issue of notice to Kenneth Lam. Mr. Shiau stated that Kenneth Lam suffers from severe dementia and resides with his son, Noel Lam, and that Noel and Monica Lam act under a power of attorney. Counsel confirmed that Kenneth Lam does not have a conservator.

The Court stated that service on agents under a power of attorney is not sufficient in lieu of personal service under the Probate Code and ordered petitioner to file proof of personal service on Kenneth Lam.

The Court continued the matter to April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
